transcript.text: We're going to start with the number of you.
Hey, Akina, how's it going?
Hi, Akina.
Hi, guys.
Hey.
All right.
How's your week, Akina?
It's good.
How's everyone?
Pretty good.
Hey, Will.
How's it, guys?
How are you guys?
Not too bad.
Hello.
Noah should be joining shortly here.
How's your guys' week going so far?
Pretty good.
Yeah, it's going good.
Good.
Yeah.
That's awesome.
We're enjoying, like, a slight break in the insane heat in New York.
Not sure where all of you are based, but...
Yeah.
Yeah.
I just, I've been in, I'm in San Francisco this week, but I'm in Rhode Island.
So similarly, was really suffering last week.
Yeah, I've been training for the New York Marathon.
So it's been particularly tricky to get outside at a time when I don't get rushed by the humidity.
3 a.m.
for that long 15 mile run.
That's right.
It's hitting again, like in the next week, right?
Like Chicago, we're just starting to pick back up at the nastiness.
Yeah, I'm heading out of town.
Luckily, I'll be in Ireland next week, which will be a bit cooler.
Going all over or staying in one place?
Flying to Dublin, and then I'll be in Galway and Limerick.
So east side and west side.
Yeah.
Have you been?
Yes, I went three years ago.
We went all over.
Nice.
Got it.
Thanks for being late.
Hey, how's it going?
Hi.
Crazy day.
Very crazy day.
No worries.
Well, I can share my screen and we can get started.
Just kind of jump in now that we're all here.
So, okay.
So, the state level pages that went live since May, we're seeing, you know, some really good numbers showing up there per week, 50 to 100 clicks.
Impressions and clicks are also growing on the editorial side.
Obviously, that production is lower with two a week since June, but we did have our best week last week.
And then city level pages are already starting to gain traction.
I know that Jakub kind of sent like a loom that talked through a little bit more of, you know, how our report works and a little bit deeper into the numbers.
But given that this is kind of like the first version of the draft would kind of, or dashboard, we'd love to kind of.
Know more about how can we better customize it to fit your needs, and how are you guys thinking about tracking overall?
Will I?
Will?
Can I can?
Okay.
So A, it's on my schedule to review the loop in two hours.
Thank you for that.
And I will provide any feedback as expedited as I can.
For me, the fact that there's a report, it's amazing.
Now we can, you know, start seeing and really, really push the needle.
So thank you for that.
Cool.
Yeah, of course.
So we can, yeah, we can stay sync on that in Slack.
And we can kind of, I know we have, Jacob and I have talked about a few updates that needs to be made.
And we'll kind of get that going.
So that, again, it's just better fits you guys' specific needs versus this is very overarching.
Thank you
Jess, would you mind just sharing the link to this notion in maybe in the growthx Slack or in the Gchat?
Yeah, for sure.
That'd be great.
Thank you.
And I'll make a note to add you guys all to this doc as well.
And I'll drop it in the channel next week before the call so that everybody's aware before we kind of jump in.
And then moving into kind of where we're at with the state of the project.
So phase one is complete, which is really exciting.
We've got 303 city level pages done by last Friday.
So those are all live.
And like I said, we're starting to see some traction there.
But as we move into phase two, we kind of need an update from like y'all side of where we're at with the Condition Hub schema.
And kind of like you guys were reviewing that.
And the goal was to kind of have.
That planned out by, you know, middle of this week.
So I just, we, do we have an update on that timeline?
Yeah.
So the Condition Hub schema will be done by end of this week.
Okay.
So by Friday.
Cool.
The 8th.
That should cover these three action items.
Cool.
And then the feedback on blog redesign edits.
That's actually on our side now, because I know you guys sent that over this morning.
So Dave is reviewing that now.
And so we're just, like, making sure that we're aligned on what that looks like.
And then once we have that, and we've already, we already have the other, like, Condition Page templates.
Which, we need, Dave, do we need any more, or will maybe, do we need...
Any more back and forth on that other like page type or has that been kind of approved and reviewed by both sides?
So the page, are we talking about the condition hub?
Yes, condition hub template.
So I think there needs to be one more round.
So I know that we're waiting on like Innerwell's version of that.
So we gave them our version and we're still waiting on your version of that so we can approve it.
And then from the blog post perspective, just doing a quick code review, but other than that, I should probably have approval by, you know, after this meeting, right after this meeting, yeah.
Cool.
Yeah, so I think we just missed this in the agenda, but last time we had talked about, yeah, getting the condition hub pages approved as well.
Yeah, so that should be end of this week from our end.
Great.

And then I think in terms of, yeah, setting up the new, like starting to set up that content migration and the timeline for that, because that, like, we can start building the, like, the Condition Hub pages and the blog once the designs are approved, but we still need the framework built on Innerwell's side to actually start publishing.
So I think it would be helpful to get a timeline for that as well.
Yeah, I, so the status right now is we, our designer pulled together sort of like full design specs in Figma.
I think, I can't remember if we shared those with you guys yet, but we're actually in the process of just finalizing what those designs look like.
I you guys initially, I think, shared kind of a template.
Or how to think about design.
And so he incorporated all of that.
That's what we're finalizing right now.
And then there's the folder structure.
We have a Google Sheet, which contains sort of like the planned folder structure.
But I think we can get back to you guys on a timeline for implementation by end of this week.
Okay, yeah, that would be great because, I mean, I think our initial plan and the initial, like, projections that we had had us publishing about 100 articles through August.

But obviously we need, like, there are two things, which are the design pieces and then the actual, like, having that format set up so that we can publish through Prismic and do similar, something similar to what we did for the city level pages.
And just start pushing through that content once we have.
But, and then I think we, will we need medical review for those at all, or how are we thinking about reviewing the condition pieces?
We will need medical reviews for those.
Okay, great.
Okay, cool.
And then as far as the editorial content for the week, Akina, I will drop these in Slack for you to review.
And then we'll get them live by end of week.
Sounds good, thank you.
Cool.
Akina, do we have that on Ben's radar for taking a look at that stuff?
How do, how do, how do we manage that in the past?
Yeah, Ben knows that every week he gets a little, a little present of articles.
Okay, great.
Thanks.
Okay, cool.
So, so yeah, I mean, I think forward from here, I think if we can.
Get those action items by end of week.
We'll have Dave send his approval on the blog by end of day.
And then we can start moving forward and getting the bridge connected and moving forward on phase two.
Awesome.
Cool.
One other question just on the Looker stuff, like the performance report that you guys pulled together.
I'm just looking at the link right now.
Is that like real time data?
So it's being refreshed from your guys, Google instance in real time?
Or is it like a sort of like static report?
It's your search console.
And so search console will be 48 to 72 hours behind.
Well, there's also GA4.
So GA4 will be more up to date any of the search console.
So I have to align to you which tabs are two days behind in which, but they're not static.
Like they are updating in real time syncing with your GA.
So, Dave, do you know off the top of your head which tabs are GSC and which are GA4, otherwise I can pull it in and send it after this call.
Yeah, so everything is GSC except for the referrals that are coming for like LLM traffic.
So this one.
So you'll see, like I'm looking at the conversion events and this week is much lower, but you're basically only through Monday, typically, because GSC is just behind.
So updating, two days behind schedule.
Yeah, this one also looks like this is Google Analytics as well.
Oh, I guess that one has to be if it's conversions, yeah.
Yeah, what is the conversion?
Like what is a good signal for like a conversion for you?
Like what is the goal?
I haven't since went in, so I'm sorry if I'm asking annoying questions, sorry about that.

You're all good, the events are all the events that your team has set up in GA4, and so there's a lot, I think that's one that we would want to pare down and know what's most important here, like it's, I don't know who's sharing the screen, but if you show the drop down on this, I don't know, there's 30 or 40, there's all these steps and things that I'm not positive what they all mean, frankly.
So this would be something that you can filter down manually, but we could update to just include the most important KPIs here.
But it's just coming from what your team has set up like.
Yes.
Okay.
Thank you.
Yeah, and if you have any other follow-ups, can, you can just let us know and we can clarify anything.
Yeah, do you guys have any other questions or updates or need clarification on anything?
Yeah, do guys have any other questions or any other other questions or questions other other questions or any any or Thank
Not on my end.
Happy to see things progressing.
Yeah, I want to start diving in the report to see if I have feedback.
So that's on my end.
Maybe Will and Akina have other things.
So thank you.
Cool.
Awesome.
Maybe next week we plan to set some time aside to really talk about like what you're looking for and how you want, like which indicators you want to really focus on so that we can think about that.
And then we'll hopefully be at the stage where we can start with the like schema approval and move on to the blog stuff.
So I don't want to wait for next week.
Is it okay that we can send you exactly everything on Slack by, will say tomorrow morning.
So like those are things.
Yeah.
And if, if, if we have, if you have questions, we can always like jump on a quick call.
Like, I don't want to wait for another weekly to talk.
Those important things, like I think it's important, if it's okay.
Yeah, that's fine.
That's great.
Yeah, cool.
Thank you.
Also, just a heads up, guys, that as I mentioned at the top of the call, I will be out of office next week, Monday to Friday.
Available on Slack if you need me, although on vacation I usually delete my Slack and email apps from my phone.
So if you guys, hopefully I'll have everything that you need on the conditions hub and also on the blog revamp by Friday, but I'll make sure that Akina and Noah have what they need.
Cool.
Thanks so much.
Yeah, in terms of that, then maybe we can just do an async update, since a lot of this is just going to be back and forth on approvals and reviews anyways.
We don't have to talk about them.
Noah and Akina may still want to meet next week.
I don't know.
I'll leave it up to them.
Let's have, let's have this in.
Let's Thank
Once it's out of the schedule, it's hard to put it back.
So let's keep it that way.
If we see there's no agenda, I don't want to spend any one time.
So let's review it next week and see if there's things to talk about.
Cool.
Sounds great.
Okay.
Well, thank you.
Thanks, guys.
Appreciate it.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Innerwell and GrowthX to discuss project progress, performance metrics, and next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Phase 1 complete: 303 city-level pages live, showing initial traction (50-100 clicks/week)](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
  - [Phase 2 timeline: Condition Hub schema due 8/11, blog redesign approval imminent](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=287.0)
  - [Performance dashboard created; team to refine KPIs and customize reporting](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [Content migration timeline needed from Innerwell for Phase 2 implementation](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=449.0)

## Topics

### Project Progress and Performance

  - [State-level pages (live since May): 50-100 clicks/week](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=141.0)
  - [Editorial content: Best week last week, despite reduced production (2/week since June)](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)
  - [City-level pages: Already gaining traction](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [Performance dashboard created; Innerwell to review and provide feedback](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [303 city-level pages completed and live as of last Friday (Phase 1 complete)](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=278.0)

### Phase 2 Planning and Timeline

  - [Condition Hub schema: To be completed by Innerwell by 8/11](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=310.0)
  - [Blog redesign edits: GrowthX reviewing, approval expected post-meeting](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=391.0)
  - [Condition Hub page templates: One more round of review needed](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=379.0)
  - [Content migration timeline: Innerwell to provide by end of week (8/11)](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
  - [Medical review needed for Condition Hub content](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)

### Technical Discussion

  - [Performance dashboard using real-time data from Google Search Console (48-72 hour delay) and GA4](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
  - [Conversion events in dashboard to be refined based on Innerwell's KPI priorities](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=726.0)
  - [Innerwell to provide detailed feedback on desired indicators and focus areas](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=764.0)

### Content Updates

  - [Two editorial pieces to be reviewed by Akina and published by end of week](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=562.0)
  - [Ben (medical reviewer) aware of weekly article review process](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=585.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Innerwell to complete Condition Hub schema by 8/11](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=310.0)
  - [GrowthX (Dave) to approve blog redesign post-meeting](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=391.0)
  - [Innerwell to provide content migration timeline by 8/11](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
  - [Innerwell to send detailed feedback on performance indicators by 8/10 morning](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=824.0)
  - [GrowthX to add team to Notion doc and share link in Slack before next meeting](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=259.0)
  - [Maintain next week's meeting; assess agenda closer to date](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [Will out of office 8/14-8/18, but will ensure all necessary info provided by 8/11](https://fathom.video/share/rUDaM-xhL6fbDHx6tQYgsLfrmh3DBSeY?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0)

action_items.description: Review Loom video about report; provide feedback ASAP
Share Notion link in Slack; add all participants to doc
Complete Condition Hub schema by Fri
Provide timeline for Condition Hub pages implementation by Fri
Drop editorial content in Slack for Akina's review
Send exact requirements for report indicators by Thu AM
